name.description: studio name, shown in the rail and project list

docTypes.description: Kinds of documents this studio works with, matched by filename glob. A matching doc gets the icon and label in the rail, its title derived per titleFrom, and a card in the New Document dialog (the filename follows the glob, the template seeds the content).

match.description: project-relative filename glob; * and ? stay within one path segment, ** spans folders

label.description: display label; defaults to the id

icon.description: 1–2 characters/emoji shown in the document rail

template.description: seed content for new docs of this type; {{title}} is substituted

actions.description: Commands surfaced in the ⌘K palette (and, for surface "selection", in the highlight toolbar). An action runs a .claude/skills skill, sends a canned prompt, or both.

id.description: stable identifier; defaults to a slug of the label

label.description: palette label (required unless a skill names it)

surface.description: what the action operates on; "selection" needs highlighted text

prompt.description: instructions sent to the writing agent (alongside or instead of the skill)

panels.description: Read-only rendered views over project files, opened as ▦ tabs from the rail or palette.

id.description: stable identifier; defaults to a slug of the title

view.description: how the source is rendered; only "markdown" today

permissions.description: Gate profile; loosening grants requires user trust in the app.

edits.description: "auto" applies the agent's file edits without per-edit approval; only takes effect after the user explicitly trusts the studio in the app

bash.description: "deny" removes the shell tool from the agent entirely (tightening never needs trust)

mcp.description: project MCP servers (.mcp.json + .claude/settings.json enablement): "none" (default) ignores them, "ask" connects them with per-tool approval, "allow" connects and auto-approves their tools; anything but "none" requires the user to trust the studio
